name: Biotin
oneliner: Real for documented deficiency. Otherwise, marketing.
headline: Biotin
dek: The supplement category most aggressively over-sold in the last decade. Biotin deficiency is genuinely rare in adults eating an unrestricted diet — and biotin supplementation in non-deficient individuals is one of the most-studied null effects in trichology. It also interferes with thyroid blood tests.
bottom: The marketing-to-evidence ratio in this category is among the worst we cover. Test before supplementing; stop before thyroid testing; do not expect transformative results in healthy adults.

evidence.note: Real, occasionally dramatic effect when biotin is genuinely low. Document deficiency before supplementing.
The 2017 Patel review remains definitive: no convincing data for benefit in biotin-replete adults.
Mixed but more positive signal than hair. Reasonable trial in nail-specific complaints.
High-dose biotin (>5 mg/d) interferes with TSH and T4 immunoassays. Stop 72 hours before thyroid tests.

forms.note: Daily-multivitamin level. Sufficient to prevent deficiency in healthy adults.
Mid-range supplement dose. Reasonable trial when deficiency genuinely suspected.
Marketing-driven dose. Lab-test interference is the main concern at this strength.

faq.q: How do I know if I'm deficient?
faq.a: Most healthy adults are not. Deficiency is associated with prolonged raw-egg-white consumption, specific genetic conditions (biotinidase deficiency), severe malnutrition, long-term anti-epileptic use, or after prolonged broth diets. Ask for a serum biotin or 3-hydroxyisovalerate test if uncertain.

faq.q: Why does it interfere with thyroid tests?
faq.a: Many immunoassay platforms use streptavidin-biotin chemistry to detect target molecules. High serum biotin competes with the assay's biotin reagents, producing false high T4/T3 and false low TSH — a pattern that mimics hyperthyroidism. Stop biotin 72 hours before any thyroid testing.

12 weeks. If hair shedding has not improved measurably (count shed hairs over a defined surface for 60 seconds; standardise the comparison), the supplement is not your bottleneck. Look at iron, ferritin, thyroid status, and androgen-driven shedding instead.
Possibly. The case-series literature on biotin and brittle nails is more positive than for hair. Reasonable 12-week trial; not a guaranteed effect.
